name: Koto
description: A simple, expressive, embeddable programming language with rich iterator support

language-family.comment: Influenced by Lua's minimalism and Rust's iterators, but with unique syntax

first-release.comment: Started in 2020 with goal of ideal Rust embedding

typing.comment: Primarily dynamic with optional type hints for parameters and return values

paradigm.comment: Supports imperative, functional via iterators/closures, and object-like patterns via metamaps

first-class-functions.comment: Functions created with |args| body syntax, can be passed and stored

closures.comment: Variables from outer scope captured by copying

pattern-matching.comment: Match expressions with destructuring, guards, list/tuple patterns with ...

error-handling.comment: Uses throw for errors; optional chaining with ? for null-safe access

async-await.comment: Not implemented; support planned for future versions

iterators-generators.comment: Major language focus; yield-based generators, rich adaptor/consumer library

module-system.comment: Prelude provides core modules; custom modules supported via import

standard-library.comment: Core library (iterator, string, list, map, io) plus optional json, yaml, toml, regex, geometry

compilation-model.comment: Supports compile() then run(), or combined evaluation

hot-reloading.comment: Module imported callback enables dependency tracking for script reloading

source-maps.comment: Runtime errors include source positions for mapping back to original code

execution-speed.comment: Claims 1.5-2x faster than Rhai; efficient bytecode VM with zero-copy string sharing

memory-overhead.comment: ~24K SLoC main crate, pure Rust with efficient data sharing

garbage-collection.comment: Uses Ptr types with configurable rc/arc features

pure-rust.comment: Pure Rust implementation with no native dependencies

api-safety.comment: Pure Rust with safe public API

idiomatic-api.comment: Good trait-based design (KotoObject), derive macros, builder patterns for settings

serde-integration.comment: koto_serde crate included as default feature

async-compatible.comment: Single-threaded by default; arc feature enables thread-safe runtime with 5-10% overhead

function-registration.comment: KotoObject trait with koto_derive macros, plus programmatic registration

type-mapping.comment: Native type sharing (Koto strings = Rust strings, lists = wrapped Vecs); serde for complex types

no-std-compatible.comment: Workspace configured with default-features = false for no_std support

wasm-compatible.comment: Online playground runs on WASM; explicit wasm-bindgen dependencies

blank-slate-mode.comment: Prelude automatically provides core library modules; no documented way to fully disable

memory-sandboxing.comment: Pure Rust implementation prevents arbitrary host memory access

syscall-restrictions.comment: stdin/stdout/stderr can be configured; no built-in file/network I/O

default-deny.comment: Prelude provides core capabilities by default

share-data.comment: Koto shares same string representation as Rust; lists are wrapped Vecs - avoids Lua<->Rust conversion overhead

custom-types.comment: KotoObject trait for implementing custom types with full integration

method-syntax.comment: Self identifier provides instance access within object methods

callbacks-events.comment: First-class functions can be stored and called as callbacks

crates-io-downloads.comment: Estimated based on ~75 monthly downloads; smaller but growing ecosystem

editor-support.comment: Tree-sitter parser, Helix built-in support (since 25.01), LSP implementation, formatter crate

example-projects.comment: Bevy game engine integration, CLI tooling

maintenance-status.comment: Regular releases, 2811 commits, active development through January 2026

documentation-quality.comment: Good language guide at koto.dev, online playground, API docs; could use more tutorials

bus-factor.comment: Primary maintainer Ian Hobson with 15 contributors

maturity.comment: Version 0.16.1; 23 releases with 15 breaking changes; actively evolving

primary-use-cases.comment: Designed for interactive systems like animation/game engines; strong iterator focus suits data transformation
